description: Fix backtest system edge cases, CLI bugs (missing sector/industry in analyze, missing --sector in watchlist-add), and improve Dockerfile security
steps: 1. In src/augur/backtest.py: Fix generate_sample_data to use a stable seed that does not depend on Python's built-in hash() (which is randomized in Python 3.12+). Replace `random.seed(hash(ticker) % 10000)` with `random.seed(sum(ord(c) for c in ticker) % 10000)` or similar deterministic computation.
2. In src/augur/backtest.py: Add guard in run_backtest for empty historical_data or forward_returns - return an empty BacktestResult early instead of proceeding with no data.

3. In src/augur/backtest.py: In _calculate_consensus_ic, handle edge case where all agents predict NEUTRAL (all signal_scores are 0) - _rank_correlation will return 0 already due to var_x=0 check, but add a quick-return if all records for a date have neutral signal.
4. In src/augur/cli.py: Fix analyze_cmd - add sector=sector, industry=industry to the manual MarketContext construction (lines ~65-75). Currently these accepted CLI options are silently dropped.
5. In src/augur/cli.py: Add --sector and --industry options to watchlist-add command to match README documentation. Pass them into the metrics dict.
6. In Dockerfile: Add a non-root user for security. After COPY commands, add `RUN useradd -r -s /bin/false augur` and `USER augur`. Adjust mkdir to happen before USER switch.

7. In Dockerfile: The HEALTHCHECK references port 8000 but CMD runs `augur api --port 8000`. This is actually consistent (both 8000), but the api command is serving a different app than dashboard.app. Change CMD to match the HEALTHCHECK expectation or vice versa. Since the HEALTHCHECK checks /health which exists on the api app, keep CMD as api but note this clearly in a comment.
8. In docker-compose.yml: Remove the deprecated `version: '3.8'` line (docker compose v2+ ignores it and shows a warning).
9. In docker-compose.yml: Add health checks to key services (dashboard, api) using the /health endpoint pattern.
acceptance_criteria: generate_sample_data produces deterministic results regardless of PYTHONHASHSEED
analyze_cmd passes sector and industry to MarketContext when user provides metrics manually
watchlist-add command accepts --sector and --industry options without error
docker-compose.yml has no version field and includes healthchecks on dashboard/api services
verification: Run pytest tests/ -v and confirm all 118+ tests pass
Run augur analyze TEST --pe 25 --sector Technology --help to verify option exists
Run augur watchlist-add --help and confirm --sector is listed
findings: All 118 tests pass. CLI --sector and --industry options verified via --help. Deterministic seed, early return guard, Dockerfile non-root user, docker-compose healthchecks all implemented successfully. Post-review fixes applied: (1) docker-compose.yml volume mounts changed from /root/.augur to /app/.augur to match non-root user, (2) Dockerfile CMD comment corrected to 'start API server', (3) generate_sample_data now uses local random.Random(seed) instance instead of global random.seed() to prevent RNG state pollution.
